We need to parse problem. We have 8 students: George, Helen, Irving, Kyle, Lenore, Nina, Olivia, Robert. Exactly six will give reports over Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday. Each day has two reports: morning and afternoon. Conditions:

1. Tuesday is the only day on which George can give a report. So George must report on Tuesday (morning or afternoon). Only Tuesday.

2. Neither Olivia nor Robert can give an afternoon report. So Olivia and Robert can only give morning reports.

3. If Nina gives a report, then on the next day Helen and Irving must both give reports, unless Nina's report is given on Wednesday. So if Nina reports on Monday or Tuesday, then the next day (Tuesday or Wednesday) must have both Helen and Irving giving reports. But if Nina reports on Wednesday, no such requirement.

We need to find which pair of students, if they give reports on the same day as each other, must give reports on Wednesday. So we consider each pair: if they are scheduled on same day, that day must be Wednesday. Which pair must be on Wednesday? We need to analyze constraints.

We need to consider all possible schedules that satisfy conditions. Determine which pair, when scheduled same day, forces that day to be Wednesday. Let's analyze.

We have 6 reports total. 3 days * 2 slots each = 6 slots. So all 6 reports fill all slots. So each day has exactly 2 reports. So we need to assign 6 students to 6 slots.

We have constraints:

- George must be on Tuesday (morning or afternoon). So Tuesday has George + another student.

- Olivia and Robert can only be morning. So they cannot be afternoon. So they must occupy morning slots on some days.

- Nina's rule: If Nina reports on Monday or Tuesday, then the next day must have both Helen and Irving. That means if Nina on Monday, then Tuesday must have Helen and Irving (both). But Tuesday already has George + someone else. So if Nina on Monday, Tuesday would need to have Helen and Irving, but Tuesday already has George. That would be 3 reports on Tuesday, impossible. So Nina cannot be on Monday. If Nina on Tuesday, then Wednesday must have Helen and Irving. That would be 2 reports on Wednesday, okay. So Nina can be on Tuesday or Wednesday. But if Nina on Tuesday, then Wednesday must have Helen and Irving. That would fill Wednesday with Helen and Irving. Then Monday would have the remaining two students (not George, not Nina, not Helen, not Irving). Also Olivia and Robert must be morning. So we need to check.

If Nina on Wednesday, no requirement. So Nina could be on Wednesday.

Thus Nina cannot be on Monday. So Nina is either Tuesday or Wednesday.

Now consider George on Tuesday. So Tuesday has George + someone else. If Nina on Tuesday, then Tuesday would have George and Nina. That satisfies Tuesday has 2 reports. Then Wednesday must have Helen and Irving. That works. Monday would have Olivia and Robert? Wait Olivia and Robert must be morning. But Monday has two slots: morning and afternoon. Olivia and Robert can only be morning, but only one morning slot per day. So they cannot both be on same day. So they must be on different days. But we have only Monday and Wednesday left for them. But Wednesday already has Helen and Irving. So cannot place Olivia or Robert on Wednesday. So they must be on Monday. But Monday has morning and afternoon. Olivia morning, Robert morning? Can't both morning. So impossible. So Nina cannot be on Tuesday. Therefore Nina must be on Wednesday.

Thus Nina is Wednesday. Good.

Now we have Wednesday: Nina + someone else. The other slot on Wednesday could be any student except constraints.

Now we need to schedule remaining students: George (Tuesday), Olivia, Robert, Helen, Irving, Kyle, Lenore, plus maybe others. We have 8 students, 6 reports. So 2 students will not report. Which ones? We need to choose.

We know Nina Wednesday. George Tuesday. Olivia and Robert morning only. So they must be morning slots on some days. They cannot be afternoon. So we need to assign them to morning slots on Monday or Tuesday or Wednesday. But Tuesday already has George + someone else. That someone else could be Olivia or Robert? But they cannot be afternoon. So if they are on Tuesday, they must be morning. But Tuesday already has George. So cannot have both morning and afternoon? Wait each day has morning and afternoon. Tuesday morning could be George or Olivia/Robert. But George must be on Tuesday, but could be morning or afternoon. So we can place George in morning or afternoon. If George is morning, then afternoon could be Olivia or Robert? But Olivia/Robert cannot be afternoon. So afternoon must be someone else. So if George morning, afternoon cannot be Olivia/Robert. So Olivia/Robert must be on Monday morning or Wednesday morning. But Wednesday morning already has Nina? Wait Nina could be morning or afternoon. But Nina is Wednesday. She could be morning or afternoon. But if Nina is morning, then afternoon slot on Wednesday must be someone else. But Olivia/Robert cannot be afternoon. So they cannot be on Wednesday afternoon. So they must be on Monday morning or Tuesday morning. But Tuesday morning may be George. So if George morning, Olivia/Robert cannot be on Tuesday. So they must be Monday morning. But only one morning slot Monday. So cannot have both. So impossible. So George cannot be morning. So George must be afternoon on Tuesday. Then Tuesday morning is free for Olivia or Robert. Good.

Thus Tuesday morning: either Olivia or Robert. Tuesday afternoon: George.

Now Olivia and Robert must be morning only. So they must occupy morning slots on Monday and Tuesday. Since Tuesday morning is one of them, Monday morning must be the other. So Monday morning: the other of Olivia/Robert. Monday afternoon: the remaining student (not Olivia/Robert). Good.

Now we have Wednesday: Nina + someone else. That someone else could be any of the remaining students: Helen, Irving, Kyle, Lenore, or the one not used yet? Let's list all students: George, Helen, Irving, Kyle, Lenore, Nina, Olivia, Robert. We have scheduled: Tuesday morning: Olivia or Robert. Tuesday afternoon: George. Monday morning: the other of Olivia/Robert. Monday afternoon: someone else. Wednesday morning/afternoon: Nina + someone else.

We need to assign Monday afternoon and Wednesday other slot to remaining students. We have 6 reports: Tuesday morning (Olivia/Robert), Tuesday afternoon (George), Monday morning (other of Olivia/Robert), Monday afternoon (X), Wednesday morning/afternoon (Nina + Y). That's 6. So X and Y are the remaining two students among Helen, Irving, Kyle, Lenore. So we need to assign them to Monday afternoon and Wednesday other slot.

Now we must satisfy that no other constraints violated. Olivia and Robert morning only satisfied. George Tuesday only day satisfied. Nina Wednesday satisfied. No other constraints. So any assignment of X and Y among the four is allowed? But we must also consider that if Nina on Wednesday, no requirement. So fine.

Thus there are many possible schedules. Now question: Which pair of students, if they give reports on the same day as each other, must give reports on Wednesday? That means: Suppose we have a pair of students. If they are scheduled on same day (i.e., same day, not necessarily same time), then that day must be Wednesday. Which pair has that property? We need to find pair such that in any valid schedule, if they are on same day, that day is Wednesday. So we need to test each pair.

(E) Olivia and Kyle. Olivia morning only. Kyle could be Monday afternoon or Wednesday other slot. If Olivia and Kyle same day, that day could be Monday? Olivia morning Monday, Kyle afternoon Monday. That would be same day Monday. So not forced to Wednesday. So pair (Olivia, Kyle) does not satisfy property.
